ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Reshmi Paneer Tikka; closest parent 'paneer food product' (FOODON_03302114) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cheese, fresh (unripened) — used as closest parent for paneer-based grilled preparations
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Reshmi Paneer Tikka; A0CKP (fresh unripened cheese) is the closest defensible parent for paneer.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Reshmi Paneer Tikka or paneer tikka preparations.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Reshmi Paneer Tikka consists of approximately 6 skewered pieces of marinated paneer with vegetables, weighing around 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: Paneer (Indian cottage cheese), grilled — nutrient composition estimated from ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017 (Paneer, raw) with adjustment for tandoor cooking and marinade ingredients
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN 2017 provides raw paneer composition (per 100 g: energy ~265 kcal, protein ~18.3 g, fat ~20.8 g, carbohydrate ~1.2 g). Reshmi Paneer Tikka includes a cream/cashew/yogurt-based marinade and tandoor grilling. Values are adapted upward for fat and energy to account for cream and cashew paste in the marinade, and adjusted for moisture loss during tandoor cooking. Final per-100g values represent the composite grilled dish as served. USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) used as cross-reference for paneer/fresh cheese baseline.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of paneer and Indian dairy-based dishes — analogous estimate based on paneer (fresh cheese) GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature on low-GI dairy foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Reshmi Paneer Tikka. Paneer is a fresh Indian cheese with very low carbohydrate content (~1–5 g per 100 g in the composite dish). The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for fresh cheese and dairy protein foods in the range of 15–30. A GI of 27 is used as a defensible analogous estimate consistent with high-protein, high-fat, low-carbohydrate dairy foods. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (27 × 5.5 × 2.0) / 100 ≈ 3.0 g, confirming very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~640 mg per serving) from marinade spices, salt, and yogurt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one serving (200 g) and request reduced salt in the marinade, as the sodium content (~640 mg per serving) represents approximately 28% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~27) and low glycemic load (~3.0 per serving) due to high protein and fat content from paneer and cream-based marinade
type2_diabetes.guidance: Reshmi Paneer Tikka has a very low glycemic index (~27) and glycemic load (~3.0 per serving), making it a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the saturated fat content (~19 g per serving) should be monitored in the context of overall dietary fat intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~9.5 g per 100 g; ~19 g per serving) from paneer, cream, and cashew paste
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content (~19 g per 200 g serving, approximately 95% of the recommended daily limit) warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption in small portions is advisable, and the dish should be balanced with low-saturated-fat foods throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Reshmi Paneer Tikka is a popular North Indian vegetarian appetiser made from cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) marinated in a rich, silky mixture of cream, cashew paste, yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, and aromatic spices including cardamom, white pepper, cumin, and garam masala. The word 'reshmi' means 'silky' in Hindi, referring to the smooth, creamy texture of the marinade that distinguishes this dish from the more spiced red-marinade variants of paneer tikka. The marinated paneer cubes are skewered together with onion and bell pepper pieces and cooked in a tandoor (traditional clay oven) at high heat, imparting a characteristic smoky char while keeping the interior soft and creamy. The dish is typically served as a starter or appetiser with mint chutney and sliced onion rings. Nutritionally, Reshmi Paneer Tikka is high in protein (~14.5 g per 100 g) and fat (~17 g per 100 g), with a very low glycemic index (~27) making it suitable for blood sugar management. The calcium content (~220 mg per 100 g) is notable due to the paneer base. The dish is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian. Clinically, the moderate sodium (~320 mg per 100 g) and high saturated fat (~9.5 g per 100 g) content are relevant considerations for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. In Singapore, this dish is served at North Indian restaurants such as Kinara at Boat Quay.

Can I eat Reshmi Paneer Tikka if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: ICMR-NIN 2017 provides a validated raw paneer composition baseline for Indian dishes. Nutrient values for the composite Reshmi Paneer Tikka dish are adapted from this source with adjustments for the cream/cashew marinade and tandoor cooking method. No exact match for Reshmi Paneer Tikka exists in any approved primary source; hence confidence is medium rather than high. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database dairy food data.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN 2017 raw paneer data (energy ~265 kcal, protein ~18.3 g, fat ~20.8 g, carbohydrate ~1.2 g per 100 g) with downward adjustment for protein and fat dilution from vegetable components (onion, bell pepper) and upward adjustment for cream and cashew paste in the marinade. Final values represent the composite grilled dish. 2. Sodium estimate (~320 mg per 100 g) is based on typical marinade salt levels for tandoor preparations; actual restaurant values may vary. 3. GI of 27 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for fresh cheese and high-protein dairy foods; no direct GI measurement for Reshmi Paneer Tikka was found in the approved literature. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (4.8), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.2), trans_fat_g (0.1), potassium_mg (180.0), calcium_mg (220.0), iron_mg (0.9), cholesterol_mg (52.0), vitamin_c_mg (8.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (85.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.04), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.18), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.3), vitamin_b6_mg (0.08), vitamin_b12_mcg (0.5), folate_mcg_dfe (18.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.3), vitamin_e_mg (0.6), phosphorus_mg (280.0), magnesium_mg (22.0), zinc_mg (1.2), selenium_mcg (8.0), and water_g (58.0) are estimated from ICMR-NIN 2017 paneer data and USDA SR Legacy fresh cheese data as cross-reference; these are not directly measured for this specific dish. 5. No -1 sentinel values remain unexplained; all micronutrient values are estimated from approved sources as noted above. 6. FoodOn code FOODON_03302114 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest parent anchors; no exact ontology code exists for Reshmi Paneer Tikka. 7. This record was sourced from field survey data (Kinara - Boat Quay); the field survey source is used only for food identity and restaurant context, not for nutrient values.
